subject: Project Management
title: Poka-Yoke: Mistake-Proofing
description: A fast breakdown of Poka-Yoke: Mistake-Proofing for builders.

hook.text: Shigeo Shingo invented poka-yoke — devices that make it physically impossible to make a mistake.

buildup.text: A USB plug that only fits one way. A car that won't start in gear. A dryer that stops when the door opens.

discovery.text: Poka-yoke assumes humans will make errors. The system should prevent those errors from causing harm.

twist.text: The cheapest poka-yoke ever: a tray with slots shaped to hold exactly the right bolts for each assembly step.

climax.text: Mistake-proofing is the most elegant quality tool: eliminate human error by design, not by discipline.

punchline.text: Don't teach people not to err. Make errors impossible.

quiz.question: What is poka-yoke?
options: Design that makes it physically impossible to make a mistake
A punishment system for factory errors
